course_title: Building Leadership Capacity: Bridging Theory and Practice in the Workplace

learning_outcomes.description: Articulate the significant issues and key elements that shape professional development for leaders.
Describe the major challenges facing education broadly and in the school context. Develop informed views about leading schools now and into the future.

Analyse the capacity of cases (and related problem-based learning strategies) as effective professional development tools for school leaders. Detail how school leaders use these to facilitate their own leadership development.
Discover and document the critical role school leaders have in the development of others, particularly in the development of other leaders.
Explore and evaluate personal and/or team leadership development needs. Design appropriate plans for addressing these needs.

assessments.task_description:
Literature Review Report

This assignment encourages students to explore the contexts in which workplace culture is nurtured. It is designed to foster an in-depth inquiry into the factors that contribute to the creation and maintenance of workplace cultures. This will be done by contextualising workplace culture through a critical literature review. You will explore workplace culture through a leadership lens. It is thus essential for you to engage with the relevant aspects of Themes 1 and 2 for the assignment. Note that it is appropriate and highly encouraged to draw on material from outside the school/educational leadership domain for this paper. It is expected that you select at least five relevant sources for this task.

Use the following question to provide a focus for your essay:

How has globalisation impacted the creation and maintenance of workplace cultures in your context (or one you are familiar with) in recent times?

Some suggestions on the topics that you can cover are as follows (these are suggestions and not compulsory options):

  • Globalisation has brought about rapid developments in technology and communications and how has this impacted your organization?
  • Globalisation has brought about changes within learning systems (ideas, values and knowledge with the changing roles of students and teachers). Do you see that in your organization and has that impacted workplace culture?
  • Globalisation has impacted the way school leaders lead organisation and plan professional development opportunities for their teachers- perhaps more networked opportunities? 
  • Globalisation has encouraged a more competitive working environment, where there is a distinct focus on accountability. Has this affected workplace culture?
  • Globalisation has resulted in a borderless society and has that impacted workplace culture? 
  • Global education has resulted in cross national boundaries, and interconnected systems on ecological, cultural, economic, political and technological grounds. Has this impacted your organization?
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assignments for this course will be submitted electronically via Blackboard and using Turnitin
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
OVERVIEW

This assessment requires you to critically analyse how leadership shapes workplace culture, drawing on your own professional context or one you know well. Your analysis should be grounded in the theoretical frameworks, learning resources, and required readings from the course, supplemented by relevant literature from the broader field of leadership and organisational studies.

You must select one option only (A, B, or C) and clearly indicate your chosen option at the beginning of your submission.


WRITTEN REPORT TASK OPTIONS — SELECT ONE

Option A: Interview-based inquiry: educator perspectives

Three to five educators from the same workplace

Conduct interviews with three (3) to five (5) educators or professionals, all drawn from the same workplace. The interviews should elicit participants’ perspectives on the extent to which leadership has contributed to, or inhibited, the development of their workplace culture. Analyse interviewees’ responses considering relevant course literature and your wider reading.

Option B: Interview-based inquiry: senior leader perspectives

Two principals or senior administrators

 As for Option A, but based on in-depth interviews with two (2) principals or senior administrators rather than a group of classroom educators. The same report structure and analytical requirements apply. The shift in participant type should be reflected in your methodological rationale and analytic framing.

Option C - Self-designed inquiry

Developed in consultation with the lecturer

 You may design your own research topic in conjunction with the lecturer. The topic must:

–    Reflect the aims and theoretical concerns of the course

–    Include a clear research component (e.g. interviews, document analysis, autoethnographic reflection)

–    Demonstrate engagement with course readings and relevant supplementary literature

–    Be submitted for lecturer approval no later than Week 6

 Students pursuing Option C must discuss their proposal with the lecturer and receive written approval before proceeding. Unapproved topics will be returned for revision.

Suggested Report Structure Options A & B

Key focus

i. Introduction ~400

Describe the issue or initiative examined and why it is significant. Provide sufficient contextual background for a reader unfamiliar with the setting.

ii. Methods~400

Describe how interviews were conducted, how questions were structured (e.g. open-ended), and justify your methodological choices with reference to qualitative research principles.

iii. Analysis~2,000

Present and interpret interviewees’ responses against relevant course literature and supplementary sources. This is the principal section of the report.

iv. Conclusion~200

Summarise key findings and offer an evaluative judgement on leadership’s impact in light of participant responses and the literature.


VIVA Q&A

Mandatory oral defence of submitted work.

The viva is a short, structured oral examination in which you will be asked to discuss and defend the key arguments, analytical choices, and conclusions presented in your written submission. It serves to authenticate your work and demonstrate that you have developed genuine command of the theoretical and empirical content of your report. The viva is not an assessment of new knowledge — it is an opportunity to show the thinking and understanding behind the work you have already submitted.

FORMAT AND LOGISTICS                             

Duration

10–15 minutes per student

Format

Individual; conducted in person or via video conference for external students (Zoom)

Conducted by

Course lecturer and/or a second academic staff member

Scheduling

Within one week of the submission deadline, you will receive a time-slot allocation

Preparation

Review your submitted report in full; be ready to discuss any section in depth. See sample Questions Areas on page 4.

Permitted materials

A printed copy of your submitted report only; no other notes or devices permitted

SAMPLE QUESTION AREAS

Questions are drawn from your submitted work and are anchored in the leadership theories and workplace culture frameworks you have engaged with. They are designed to probe the depth of your theoretical understanding and how you have applied it to your chosen context. You should be prepared to:

–    Explain the leadership theory or model you drew on (e.g. transformational, distributed, instructional, or servant leadership) and articulate why it was the most appropriate lens for understanding culture in your chosen context.

–    Discuss how the leaders in your study shaped — or failed to shape — workplace culture, and which specific leadership behaviours, values, or practices your participants identified as most influential.

–    Identify where your participants’ accounts aligned with or diverged from the theoretical claims in the literature — for example, where a leader’s stated values did not translate into the cultural experience of staff — and explain what that gap suggests.

–    Describe the cultural conditions in your workplace — such as trust, collaboration, accountability, or psychological safety — and explain how these were enabled or constrained by the leadership practices you observed or were told about.

–    Reflect on what your analysis reveals about the relationship between leadership and culture in your context and consider what a leader in that setting could do differently to better nurture a productive and inclusive workplace culture.

Use of AI/MT to support or inform assessment

If you use AI and/or machine translation (MT) technologies in any way in preparing the research presentation in this assessment task, you must provide specific details about how it was used. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute academic misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Guidelines about conventions for appropriate referencing and acknowledgement, including an example coversheet to provide details of use, are available here: Overview - AI tools for assignments - Library Guides at University of Queensland Library

However, the [Viva Q&A section] of this research presentation is designed to protect academic integrity and assess your understanding of course concepts. You will be expected to satisfactorily respond to pertinent questions about your research (to discuss and defend the key arguments, analytical choices, and conclusions) presented in your written submission. spontaneously, without the use of AI or other materials or tools]. You must only use the approved materials and tools for the [Viva Q&A section] of the task. Use of prohibited materials in this section of the task may be investigated and treated as academic misconduct.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 1: Roots of leadership capacity

THEME ONE: Building Leadership Capacity This week is the introduction to the course, and we will be delving into the roots of building leadership capacity. This session will provide an excellent overview of the fascinating field's primary theories and topics, and reveals the new and upcoming issues and questions. We will also explore concrete study materials (e.g., cases, suggestions for further reading, discussion topics, and links to videos).


Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 1: Roots of leadership capacity THEME ONE: Building Leadership Capacity This week is the introduction to the course, and we will be delving into the roots of building leadership capacity. This session will provide an excellent overview of the fascinating field's primary theories and topics, and reveals the new and upcoming issues and questions. We will also explore concrete study materials (e.g., cases, suggestions for further reading, discussion topics, and links to videos).

learning_activities.topic:
Week 2: Defining Leadership Capacity

In the second week, our focus will be on building and sustaining leadership capacity for school improvement. You will be provided with updated classic content that forms the basis of our current understanding of leadership study and practice, and it addresses topics important for today and tomorrow's leaders in diverse organizations to promote innovation and purpose. You will explore both the extensive scientific evidence and practical examples important in understanding what leaders need to "do," how leaders develop the skills and abilities to "do what they must do", and also for identifying ways to assess the effectiveness of that "doing."


Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 2: Defining Leadership Capacity In the second week, our focus will be on building and sustaining leadership capacity for school improvement. You will be provided with updated classic content that forms the basis of our current understanding of leadership study and practice, and it addresses topics important for today and tomorrow's leaders in diverse organizations to promote innovation and purpose. You will explore both the extensive scientific evidence and practical examples important in understanding what leaders need to "do," how leaders develop the skills and abilities to "do what they must do", and also for identifying ways to assess the effectiveness of that "doing."

learning_activities.topic:
Week 3: Leadership capacity in schools

This week's focus is on exploring the journey of school improvement. It will include an analysis of patterns of change in school improvement processes and learning outcomes. There is a rich and extensive examination of what leadership is, how it happens, and why it matters.

Guest lecturer: Ms Karen Fox/ Mr Norman Hunter


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 3: Leadership capacity in schools This week's focus is on exploring the journey of school improvement. It will include an analysis of patterns of change in school improvement processes and learning outcomes. There is a rich and extensive examination of what leadership is, how it happens, and why it matters. Guest lecturer: Ms Karen Fox/ Mr Norman Hunter

learning_activities.topic:
Week 4: Teacher leadership capacity

Studying the impact of leadership on student outcomes: An analysis of the differential effects of leadership types. In this session, we will explore "The Principals' Standard and the Teachers' Standards at the Highly Accomplished and Lead Teacher stages. This will detail what effective school leaders should know, understand and do at a range of career stages. This session will help to recognise the breadth and complexity of school leadership and teacher leadership, and are important resources when considering the leadership capabilities required at different career stages and across different contexts. There will also be discussions based on other international standards of leadership as a comparison.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 4: Teacher leadership capacity Studying the impact of leadership on student outcomes: An analysis of the differential effects of leadership types. In this session, we will explore "The Principals' Standard and the Teachers' Standards at the Highly Accomplished and Lead Teacher stages. This will detail what effective school leaders should know, understand and do at a range of career stages. This session will help to recognise the breadth and complexity of school leadership and teacher leadership, and are important resources when considering the leadership capabilities required at different career stages and across different contexts. There will also be discussions based on other international standards of leadership as a comparison.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 5: Organisational culture and the workplace

THEME TWO: Discovering relationships between Leadership and Workplace. Students will gain a better understanding of developing inclusive education systems and the role of organisational cultures and leadership. We will in this session examine the role of leaders in creating thriving workplace cultures. We will examine different countries' practices and how this have impacted the establishment of an inclusive education system.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 5: Organisational culture and the workplace THEME TWO: Discovering relationships between Leadership and Workplace. Students will gain a better understanding of developing inclusive education systems and the role of organisational cultures and leadership. We will in this session examine the role of leaders in creating thriving workplace cultures. We will examine different countries' practices and how this have impacted the establishment of an inclusive education system.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 6: Emotional Intelligence and the workplace- Flipped Classroom

Emotional intelligence: Implications for personal, social, academic, and workplace success. We will examine "Why EQ Is Important for Success?" and "Why EQ Matters in the Workplace?".

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: Week 6: Emotional Intelligence and the workplace- Flipped Classroom
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 6: Emotional Intelligence and the workplace- Flipped Classroom Emotional intelligence: Implications for personal, social, academic, and workplace success. We will examine "Why EQ Is Important for Success?" and "Why EQ Matters in the Workplace?".

learning_activities.topic:
Week 7: Culture, leadership and the workplace

The focus for this week is on Principal as cultural leaders. We will examine the impact of school culture and leadership on teachers' learning in the workplace.

Guest lecturer: Dr Vicente Reyes, Associate Professor, University of Bristol.

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 7: Culture, leadership and the workplace The focus for this week is on Principal as cultural leaders. We will examine the impact of school culture and leadership on teachers' learning in the workplace. Guest lecturer: Dr Vicente Reyes, Associate Professor, University of Bristol.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 8: Theory policy, practice: Workplace Leader

THEME THREE: Critique of the Theory and Practice of Leadership and Workplace The focus for this week is on leading and managing people in education. There are disconnects between theory and practice, resulting in disengaged employees, ruined careers, and lower organisational performance. This session will examine and discuss what can be done to mitigate poor leaderships negative effect on organisations and careers. This session will look at some case analysis forcing students to confront the differences between what should be done and what is actually done. This session will be a flipped classroom session and students will engage in independent case analysis online.

Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 8: Theory policy, practice: Workplace Leader THEME THREE: Critique of the Theory and Practice of Leadership and Workplace The focus for this week is on leading and managing people in education. There are disconnects between theory and practice, resulting in disengaged employees, ruined careers, and lower organisational performance. This session will examine and discuss what can be done to mitigate poor leaderships  negative effect on organisations and careers. This session will look at some case analysis forcing students to confront the differences between what should be done and what is actually done. This session will be a flipped classroom session and students will engage in independent case analysis online.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 9: Supervision and leadership in the workplace

This week, we will be exploring the role of supervision and leadership. It will introduce students to the essential elements of leading people at work. This includes both the practical exploration of tools and techniques that every leader needs, as well as the critical aspects of being a leader. Here, participants will be asked to reflect on their own behaviours, and in doing so, establish a level of self-awareness needed to be successful in any managerial capacity.

Guest lecturer: Professor Robin Shields.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_title: Week 9: Supervision and leadership in the workplace

It will introduce students to the essential elements of leading people at work. This includes both the practical exploration of tools and techniques that every leader needs, as well as the critical aspects of being a leader. Here, participants will be asked to reflect on their own behaviours, and in doing so, establish a level of self-awareness needed to be successful in any managerial capacity. Guest lecturer: Professor Robin Shields.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 10: Theory and practice of leadership

We will focus on the role of leadership in the pursuit of Indigenous excellence in educational settings. Information will be shared on a pilot study, Doing things right way: Dimensions of excellence in Indigenous education in Queensland secondary schools. This study explores what Indigenous excellence is or could be in Indigenous education. This is intended to inform a model of leadership that is grounded in concepts underpinned by excellence.

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 10: Theory and practice of leadership We will focus on the role of leadership in the pursuit of Indigenous excellence in educational settings. Information will be shared on a pilot study, Doing things right way: Dimensions of excellence in Indigenous education in Queensland secondary schools. This study explores what Indigenous excellence is or could be in Indigenous education. This is intended to inform a model of leadership that is grounded in concepts underpinned by excellence.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 11: Reflections: Theory & Practice Leadership

We will continue to examine some case study analysis. We have an opportunity to think deeply and to reflect on unique approaches in schools and organisations for navigating and possibly generating new resources, as well as considering new ways to address engagement and learning in schools. Students are invited to interrogate the challenges within education and examine the potential of educational research to reconnect communities globally.

Guest speakers: Mr Tom Dennis - SJI International School (Singapore).

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05

Students are invited to interrogate the challenges within education and examine the potential of educational research to reconnect communities globally. Guest speakers:  Mr Tom Dennis - SJI International School (Singapore).

learning_activities.topic:
Week 12: Theory & practice of Leadership

Lecture will focus on discussions on Assessment 2- How to go about doing the task and tips for navigating through the set task.

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05

learning_activities.topic:
Week 13: Independent Study/Preparation for Ass 2.

Students will use this time for independent work to prepare for Assessment 2. They can also arrange for consultation sessions with their tutors.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
